content: Thyroid disorders encompass hypothyroidism (underactive thyroid) and hyperthyroidism (overactive thyroid). Hypothyroidism: most commonly Hashimoto's thyroiditis (autoimmune), also post-radioactive iodine, surgery, medications (amiodarone, lithium), iodine deficiency. Symptoms: fatigue, weight gain, cold intolerance, constipation, depression, menorrhagia. Hyperthyroidism: Graves' disease (autoimmune with TSH receptor antibodies) most common, toxic multinodular goiter, toxic adenoma, thyroiditis. Symptoms: weight loss, heat intolerance, palpitations, tremor, anxiety, diarrhea. Thyroid hormones (T4, T3) regulate metabolism, growth, development via nuclear receptors. TSH from pituitary provides sensitive feedback regulation. Subclinical disease: abnormal TSH with normal free T4.
